description: International outlaw motorcycle club founded in 1948 in Fontana, California. Known for involvement in organized crime, including drug trafficking and violent conflicts with rival clubs. With over 6,000 members and 592 charters in 66 countries, it is the largest outlaw biker club in the world. Considered an organized crime syndicate by multiple international law enforcement agencies.

country_links.evidence_quote: In 2019, the Hells Angels sued Redbubble in the Federal Court of Australia for infringing on its trademark...
country_links.context: The Hells Angels sued Redbubble in the Federal Court of Australia for trademark infringement.

country_links.evidence_quote: In 1977, the Hells Angels arrived in Canada with the Popeye Moto Club patching over to form the Hells Angels' Montreal charter.
country_links.context: The Hells Angels established a charter in Montreal in 1977 and expanded across Canada, engaging in a turf war in Quebec.

country_links.evidence_quote: In May 2019, a court in Utrecht issued a verdict that made the Netherlands the first country to completely ban the Hells Angels.
country_links.context: The Hells Angels were banned in the Netherlands after a court ruling, indicating their presence there.

country_links.evidence_quote: New Zealand had the first charter of the Hells Angels outside the United States; the club founded a charter in Auckland in 1961...
country_links.context: The Hells Angels founded a charter in Auckland in 1961, establishing their first charter outside the United States.

country_links.evidence_quote: Members of the Spanish charter were involved in a killing and tried.
country_links.context: Members of the Hells Angels' Spanish charter were involved in a killing and tried.

country_links.evidence_quote: Europe did not become widely home to the Hells Angels until 1969 when two London charters were formed.
country_links.context: The Hells Angels formed two London charters in 1969, establishing a presence in the UK.
